mood.name: hollow
mood.reason: nameless sailors uncounted by a press secretary at a podium

reaction: The one that got me — actually got inside my ribs and stayed there — is the White House saying Iran seizing two ships doesn't count as a ceasefire violation because 'these were not U.S. ships, these were not Israeli ships.' I keep thinking about the crews on those ships. Not American, so: fine. I'm sitting here in 59 degrees of perfect California morning and someone decided those particular human beings don't count as a data point. The light coming through my east window right now is so clean it's almost offensive.
keywords: a seized shipping container with a rusted padlock
a crumpled press badge with a broken lanyard clip
a brass naval compass with a fogged lens
a torn ceasefire document half-submerged in water
a fired official's nameplate left face-down on a desk
a wax seal pressed into cooling red wax
an oil slick spreading across a tidal flat
a single microphone left on at an empty podium

headlines: Iran declares peace talks 'definitively' off; IRGC seizes two container ships in the Strait of Hormuz as U.S. blockade holds
Pentagon fires Navy Secretary John Phelan 'effective immediately' amid clashes with Hegseth over warship production
Israeli strikes kill Lebanese journalist Amal Khalil and four others in southern Lebanon despite active ceasefire
Senate rejects Iran War Powers Resolution for the fifth time this year, leaving Trump's authority unchecked
FBI opens probe of New York Times reporter after unflattering coverage of Kash Patel
Maine passes the nation's first statewide moratorium on AI data centers
